name: Boca del Infierno – circular by the Calzada Romana and the Senda de Los Ganchos – Valle de Hecho
name_original: Boca del Infierno – circular por la Calzada Romana y la Senda de Los Ganchos – Valle de Hecho
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De Hecho offers access to a diverse natural landscape within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by dense forests, deep gorges, and high-mountain peaks. The region is part of the Valles Occidentales Natural Park, featuring the impressive Selva de Oza with its Beech, Silver Fir, and Pine forests, and the Aragón Subordán river. Glacial lakes, known as ibones, and prominent peaks like Castillo de Acher contribute to the varied terrain. This area provides a range of hiking experiences from valley walks to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Valle De Hecho The most popular hiking route is Castillo d'Acher Trail, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to the iconic Castillo de Acher peak, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Boca del Infierno – circular by the Calzada Romana and the Senda de Los Ganchos – Valle de Hecho, a moderate 4.9 miles (8.0 km) path. This route explores the dramatic Boca del Infierno gorge and ancient Roman paths. Local hikers also love the Siresa - Barranco del Hospital Loop, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through forested areas and along the Barranco del Hospital, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Valle De Hecho is defined by dense forests, glacial lakes, and dramatic mountain peaks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy valley strolls to difficult high-altitude ascents. The routes in Valle De Hecho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 160 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Hecho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De Hecho?
A: Valle De Hecho offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 45 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from leisurely walks to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What do hikers enjoy most about the trails in Valle De Hecho?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Valle De Hecho, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscape, including dense forests, dramatic gorges, and stunning high-mountain views. The well-preserved environment and variety of routes are often highlighted.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valle De Hecho?
A: The best time for hiking in Valle De Hecho is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October), when the weather is mild and trails are clear of snow. Summer offers warmer temperatures, ideal for exploring high-mountain areas. For those interested in winter activities, the region also provides opportunities for snowshoeing and ski mountaineering.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Valle De Hecho?
A: Yes, Valle De Hecho has several easy hiking trails, with 9 routes specifically categorized as easy. These often include leisurely strolls through valley floors or forested areas, perfect for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. The region's diverse landscape ensures options for all abilities.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Valle De Hecho?
A: Hikes in Valle De Hecho traverse a diverse range of terrain. You can expect to encounter dense Beech, Silver Fir, and Pine forests, deep gorges like the Boca del Infierno, extensive high-mountain pastures such as Guarrinza Meadows, and rocky peaks. The region also features unique meandering river valleys and glacial lakes.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Valle De Hecho?
A: Absolutely. Valle De Hecho is home to 13 difficult routes, offering significant elevation gains and rugged terrain for experienced trekkers. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932472165/castillo-d-acher-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo d'Acher Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to the iconic Castillo de Acher peak, providing expansive views and a demanding climb.

Q: Can I find any glacial lakes or ibones on hikes in Valle De Hecho?
A: Yes, Valle De Hecho is renowned for its beautiful high-mountain glacial lakes, known locally as ibones. You can hike to stunning examples like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Acherito&lt;/a&gt;, one of the few natural ibones remaining in the Pyrenees, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanés Lake&lt;/a&gt;. These offer picturesque destinations for a rewarding hike.

Q: Are there any notable natural landmarks to see while hiking in Valle De Hecho?
A: Valle De Hecho boasts several impressive natural landmarks. The dramatic Boca del Infierno gorge, the ancient Selva de Oza forest, and the whimsical Valle de Aguas Tuertas with its meandering river are all prominent features. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3123830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanchèse Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4929721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llano de Tacheras&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valle De Hecho?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938382066/boca-del-infierno-circular-by-the-calzada-romana-and-the-senda-de-los-ganchos-valle-de-hecho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca del Infierno – circular by the Calzada Romana and the Senda de Los Ganchos – Valle de Hecho&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8 km loop that explores the dramatic gorge and ancient paths. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943575834/siresa-barranco-del-hospital-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siresa - Barranco del Hospital Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.4 km trail through forested areas.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while hiking in Valle De Hecho?
A: Valle De Hecho is a sanctuary for diverse wildlife, making it an excellent area for wildlife spotting. Hikers may encounter Brown Bears, Chamois, and Marmots. The region is also a Special Bird Protection Zone (ZEPA), with over 200 bird species, including Lammergeiers, Golden Eagles, and Wallcreepers.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming after a hike in Valle De Hecho?
A: Yes, the rivers in Valle De Hecho offer natural swimming pools, providing refreshing spots for a dip after a hike during warmer months. The pristine waters of the Aragón Subordán river and its tributaries create inviting areas for wild swimming.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Valle De Hecho?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead can be limited in this mountainous region, the main villages within Valle De Hecho, such as Hecho and Siresa, are typically accessible by local bus services. From these points, some trailheads might be within walking distance or require a short taxi ride. It's advisable to check local transport schedules in advance.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Valle De Hecho?
A: Parking is generally available at or near popular trailheads and key access points within Valle De Hecho, especially in areas like Selva de Oza and near the villages of Hecho and Siresa. Designated parking areas are often provided to accommodate hikers. Always check for specific parking instructions or fees for individual routes before your trip.
